1995: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema was given to the legendary Italian actress Gina Lollobrigida.
One of the guests at the festival was American actress Mia Farrow.
1996: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema was awarded to Hollywood actor Gregory Peck.
The festival was also visited by Whoopi Goldberg who has employed her comedy talent in such films as the Oscar-winning Ghost and the box-office hit comedy Sister Act.
1997: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema was awarded to director Miloš Forman, whose films are an important part of both Czech and American cinematographic heritage.
Audiences were delighted to see the charming actress Salma Hayek.
1998: The big star of the 33rd Karlovy Vary festival was American actor Michael Douglas, who received the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
The festival atmosphere was also livened up by the presence of the legendary Lou Reed.
1999: Franco Zeffirelli has always straddled the arts of theatre and film. His love of theratre led him to filmmaking in which he stood out tanks in particular to his adaptations of Shakespeare’s plays. The director accepted a Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
The star of such films as Natural Born Killers and The People vs. Larry Flynt, Woody Harrelson caused a lot of excitement among film fans at the festival.
2000: Director Carlos Saura received the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema at this year’s festival. Along with Almodóvar, Saura is one of Spain’s most well-established filmmakers. Throughout much of his career he collaborated with Geraldine Chaplin.
The festival was also visited by the immensely talented actor Edward Norton, who viewers know from Forman’s The People vs. Larry Flynt, among others.
2001: Ben Kingsley: The Academy Award and two-time Golden Globes winner has brought many of cinema’s iconic characters to the silver screen - Itzhak Stern, Gandhi, and Georges Méliès among others. At this year’s festival he received the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
A number of rising stars have been guests at the festival – among them future Hollywood superstar Scarlett Johansson.
2002: One of the important guests at the 37th festival was the young actress Kiera Knightley, who came to present the film Bend It Like Beckham.
Free spirited director Kim Ki-duk has been a guest at the festival on a number of occasions. At the 37th KVIFF he took part in an extensive retrospective of his work.
2003: The 38th festival was honoured by the attendance of charismatic actor Morgan Freeman, who received a Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
Crystal Globes for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema were awarded for the lifetime achievements of Jiří Menzel and Stephen Frears.
2004: The range of characters that Harvey Keitel has played is truly wide ranging. He has portrayed brutal and violent thugs, as well as sensitive and loving husbands and fathers. At this year’s festival he received the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
A Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema also went to director Roman Polanski, the creator of such films as Chinatown, Rosemary’s Baby and The Pianist.
This year’s festival was attended by none other than the idol of young film fans, actor Elijah Wood.
2005: Giant of international film Robert Redford received the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema. For the occasion of such an important filmmaker’s presence at the festival, five films that he directed or starred in were screened. The festival opened with the legendary 1969 film Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid.
Star of European film, Ingmar Bergman’s young muse, author and UNICEF ambassador. Liv Ullmann accepted the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
2006: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema was received by actor Andy Garcia, who nonetheless attended the festival as a director. His debut film The Lost City had its European premiere at the festival.
Eminent director of unbridled imagination Terry Gilliam presented the film Tideland at the festival.
2007: The successful actor, director and producer Danny DeVito accepted the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema at the festival. Czech audiences may know him not only from Miloš Forman’s One Flew over the Cuckoo’s Nest, but also from many comedies and as producer of the films Pulp Fiction and Erin Brockovich.
The Karlovy Vary festival was also attended by Oscar winner Renée Zellweger, who presented the film Miss Potter.
2008: Two-time Oscar winner Robert De Niro added a Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema to his collection. The presence of a living legend was a big event at the festivals.
Christopher Lee has played many a villain, but always with undeniably elegance. It was unsurprisingly with that same air of elegance that he took the Festival President’s Award
2009: The Festival President’s Award was happily accepted by actor Antonio Banderas, who audiences know as a frequent collaborator with director Pedro Almodóvar and a charismatic star of Hollywood films
Master of supporting roles John Malkovich enthralled audiences with his presence and took home the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema
2010: British actor Jude Law received the Festival President’s Award.
The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema was received by director Nikita Michalkov.
2011: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema was accepted by British actress Judi Dench, known for her roles as Queen Elizabeth I and James Bond’s legendary boss, M.
Actor John Turturro won the Festival President’s Award.
2012: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema was received with elegance by Helen Mirren.

Another big star of the festival was American actress Susan Sarandon. she took home the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema and personally presented the film Jeff Who Lives at Home.
2013: In the context of modern cinematography, director Oliver Stone is a figure who has eluded the traditional standards of evaluation. The director of Platoon and many other films received the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema at the festival.
The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema was accepted by energetic actor and passionate dancer John Travolta, who became a darling of festival audiences.
2014: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema was enthusiastically received by actor, director and producer Mel Gibson.
The American director of such films as Mzda strachu and The Exorcist received the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema at the festival.
2015: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.

Guests at the 50th Karlovy Vary IFF also included actor Harvey Keitel, winner of the Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema at the 2004 KVIFF. At this year’s festival he accompanied Paolo Sorrentino’s film Youth , which won the audience award.
2016: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
2017: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
2018: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
2019: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
The Festival President’s Award for Contribution to Czech Cinema.
2021: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
Actor, producer and musician Johnny Depp introduced the film Crock of Gold: A Few Rounds with Shane MacGowan and the film Minamata.
The Festival President’s Award for Contribution to Czech Cinema.
2022: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
The Festival President’s Award for Contribution to Czech Cinema.
2023: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
The Festival President’s Award for Contribution to Czech Cinema.
2024: The Festival President’s Award for Contribution to Czech Cinema.
2025: The Crystal Globe for Outstanding Artistic Contribution to World Cinema.
